Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(etl): remove spurious _parent key and extra columns from clientes_enderecos

- _process_categorias: row["_parent"] had single underscore, pg_upsert only
  filters double-underscore keys, so _parent was included in INSERT column
  list causing "column _parent does not exist" DB error. Since the value is
  never used (pass-2 re-queries MySQL directly), simply pop parent_id and
  set to None without saving to a temp key.

- transform_cliente_endereco_from_cliente: is_principal and created_at were
  included in the returned dict but neither column exists in the Supabase
  is_clientes_enderecos schema (confirmed via column_mapping.json). Removed
  both to prevent INSERT errors for derived addresses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial implementation: CS:GO competitive matchmaking system

Full community matchmaking stack for CS:GO Legacy (post-2023):

- install.sh: Interactive wizard (2590 lines) supporting Ubuntu/Debian,
  CentOS/RHEL/Fedora, Arch Linux; guides GSLT setup, auto-detects IP,
  generates all configs, installs CS:GO + Sourcemod + plugins, sets up
  systemd services, validates installation

- database/schema.sql: 10 mm_* tables + views (queue, matches, players,
  ELO history, seasons, map pool, GSLT pool, port pool, bans)

- matchmaker/: Python daemon with modular ABC backends
  - interfaces/: QueueBackend, ServerBackend, RankingBackend, NotificationBackend
  - backends/: MySQL polling, Docker orchestration, ELO ranking (18 tiers,
    snake-draft team balancing, dynamic spread), Discord webhooks, noop
  - factory.py: backend selection from config.env
  - Full unit tests for ELO, queue manager, Docker backend

- lobby-server/: SourcePawn plugins (SM 1.11+)
  - csgo_mm_queue.sp: !queue/!leave/!status/!rank/!top/!stats + ready check
  - csgo_mm_notify.sp: HUD messages, periodic announcements
  - csgo_mm_admin.sp: !mm_forcestart/!mm_ban/!mm_setelo etc.
  - include/csgo_mm.inc: shared constants, rank names, stock helpers

- match-server/: Docker image (cm2network/csgo:sourcemod base)
  - csgo_mm_match.sp: whitelist gate, stat tracking, match-end redirect
  - entrypoint.sh: generates databases.cfg + mm_match.cfg from env vars
  - Competitive 5v5 config: MR30, OT, 128-tick, no bots

- web-panel/: Flask app with dark CS:GO theme
  - Leaderboard, player profiles (SVG ELO graph), match scoreboard
  - REST API (/api/queue/count, /player, /leaderboard, /matches)
  - Full CSS with rank badges, scoreboard, responsive layout

- scripts/: backup.sh, restore.sh, health_check.sh (--json mode),
  update_server.sh
- docker-compose.yml: MySQL + matchmaker + webpanel services

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Navigate immediately with router.push() without waiting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite modal as scrollable viewport overlay (Tailwind UI pattern)

Instead of trying to scroll a child element (which iOS PWA blocks),
the fixed root overlay itself is the scroll container with
overflow-y-auto. Content is a normal flex child with no max-height
or overflow — it grows naturally and the viewport scrolls around it.
This is the proven Headless UI / Tailwind UI modal pattern.

commit.message:
v54: Kritische Bugfixes – Duplikate, Delete, Fremd-Einträge

- Fix 1: UUID-Migration in load() nutzt isUUID() statt /^\d/ Regex
  → /^\d/ matchte ca. 37% aller gültigen UUIDs (Hex-Ziffern 0-9)
  → Verhindert exponentielles Duplikat-Wachstum bei jedem App-Start

- Fix 2: syncNow() Reihenfolge korrigiert: Push vor Pull
  → Lokale Änderungen werden zuerst hochgeladen, dann Remote-Stand geladen

- Fix 3: deleteEntry() ruft syncPush() statt syncNow() auf
  → syncPull() würde gelöschte Einträge sofort wiederherstellen

- Fix 4: syncPull() – user_id-Filter, deletedIds mergen, ID-Dedup
  → Fremde Einträge werden clientseitig gefiltert (Sicherheitsnetz)
  → Lokale Tombstones bleiben bei Pull erhalten (nicht überschrieben)
  → Server-seitige ID-Duplikate werden dedupliziert

- Fix 5: deduplicateLocalEntries() – content-basierter One-Time Cleanup
  → Bereinigt bestehende ~200 Duplikate nach dem ersten syncPull
  → Tombstones werden via syncPush() auf den Server propagiert

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SSH pool + combined command strategies to benchmark

Strategy C: SSH connection pool (round-robin, configurable size)
Strategy D: Combined write+exec+cleanup in single SSH call
New --ssh-pool-size flag (default: 8)

commit.message:
Add WSL onboarding flow and harden CLI-app IPC (#63)

* Add WSL onboarding flow and harden CLI-app IPC

WSL Onboarding:
- Add platform-check and platform-setup CLI subcommands that detect
  WSL2 availability, virtualization, distro health on Windows
- Add platform step in app onboarding that auto-advances on non-Windows,
  guides WSL install/reboot/BIOS on Windows
- Proactive detection before agent creation instead of crashing mid-setup

IPC Hardening:
- Add 120s timeout to all CLI calls, 600s for auth (kills hung processes)
- Strip ANSI escape codes (CSI, OSC, charset) in collect_lines
- Improve error extraction: find last "error:" line, fall back to last
  non-empty line, guard against empty stdout in run_json
- Add Timeout variant to ErrorCode

CLI UX:
- Bare "vesta" prints friendly welcome instead of clap help
- "vesta status" shows contextual hints (next action to take)
- Remove redundant wsl_available() in favor of wsl_status_output()
- platform_setup builds result JSON inline instead of re-running checks

* Bump version to 0.1.42

Fix ItemInventoryAdd qbXML: use SalesTaxCodeRef, fix element order

and reorder elements to match qbXML SDK spec. Fixes QB XML parse error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: parse agent JSON response in result command (#98)

Instead of hardcoding status as "completed", parse the JSON payload from
the agent's MCP result tool to extract status, message_count, and
result_text fields. This ensures klausctl result displays accurate agent
status (busy/idle/completed/error) with message count in both text and
JSON output modes.

Apply the same parsing fix to the MCP tool handler (handleResult) so MCP
clients also get accurate data. Show a progress hint when the agent is
busy suggesting the user re-run the command.

Closes #79

Co-authored-by: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add wait tool and fix background process polling loop

Models called check_background_process 50+ times in a tight loop
when waiting for long commands. Added a `wait` tool (max 30s sleep)
so models can properly throttle polling. Also added no_output_checks
counter with hard refusal after 5 consecutive empty checks, elapsed
time display, and fixed loop detection to match all model formats.

Co-authored-by: mail <mail@janjaap.de>

bulkUpdatePermissions skip behavior (from previous commit) unchanged.

Made-with: Cursor

This reverts commit 490cc059ed95a20fa1043199bc09be5567875106.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: remove ai slop bs

* fix: remove ai slop bs

* fix: remove ai slop bs

* fix: use pnpm deploy for api runtime image

Switch Docker runtime packaging to pnpm deploy output, rebuild native modules in builder, and align runtime base/user tooling to avoid native module load failures in CI and container startup.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: prevent null clientSecret overwriting on OIDC config partial updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: WIP

* fix: claude launch.json

* feat(ATT-240): configurable cookie SameSite policy with OIDC state isolation

- Add user-configurable `cookieSameSite` setting (lax/strict/none) stored
  in the DB; `none` auto-falls-back to `lax` with a warning when not HTTPS
- Replace implicit express-session OIDC state with a dedicated signed
  `oidc-state` cookie (HMAC-SHA256, single-use, always SameSite=Lax)
  so users can choose SameSite=Strict without breaking SSO IdP redirects
- Add `OidcCookieStateStore` NestJS service wiring it into the OIDC strategy
- Explicitly configure express-session cookie (SameSite=Lax, secure, httpOnly)
  for SAML flows that still rely on it
- Remove deprecated `FRONTEND_URL` env var; consolidate on single `ATTRACCESS_URL`
  and use Vite dev-proxy so frontend always hits the same origin
- Remove hardcoded `getConfig()` from `CookieConfigService`; expose
  `getCookieName()` instead; fix all callers in `AuthController`
- Add DB migrations: remove `frontend_url` setting, add `cookie_same_site` setting
- Add/update exhaustive unit tests for cookie-security, cookie-config and
  oidc-cookie-state-store (32 new tests, full SameSite x URL matrix)
- Update AppSettingsForm with Select UI for SameSite policy (en + de i18n)
- Regenerate api-client and react-query-client after settings API changes
- Fix permissionsForm: treat all permissions as SSO-managed when providers
  are set but no granular ssoManagedPermissionKeys are provided
- Fix stale spec mocks: auth.controller (getCookieName), oidc.strategy
  (4th stateStore arg), license.service and email.service (single URL)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update api client

* fix(ATT-240): guard against missing backend_url in RemoveFrontendUrlSetting migration down()

backendUrl[0].value crashed on a fresh DB (e.g. CI) where no backend_url
row exists. Use optional chaining with an empty-string fallback, and
INSERT OR IGNORE to avoid duplicate-key errors if down() is called twice.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ATT-240): allow saving non-SSO-mapped permissions for partial SSO users

Previously any SSO permission mapping on a provider caused all permission
edits for that user to be blocked with UserPermissionsManagedBySSO, even for
permissions that weren't mapped to any SSO role.

Replace the all-or-nothing block with per-permission filtering:
getSsoManagedPermissions() returns only the specific permission keys that have
SSO role mappings configured. updatePermissions and bulkUpdatePermissions now
skip only those specific keys, allowing unmapped permissions to be saved
normally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ATT-240): add missing canManageBilling to updatePermissions DTO and handler

canManageBilling was present on SystemPermissions and accepted by the frontend
but absent from UpdateUserPermissionsDto and both controller handlers, causing
the field to be silently dropped on every save.

Add canManageBilling to the DTO, handle it in updatePermissions and
bulkUpdatePermissions (with SSO-managed filtering), and cover it with a test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(ATT-240): add regression guard for missing SystemPermissions fields in updatePermissions

Add a test that turns every SystemPermissions key off and asserts all are
persisted, so any future permission added to the entity but omitted from
UpdateUserPermissionsDto or the controller handler will immediately fail.

Also imports SystemPermissions into the spec for typed key enumeration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ATT-240): invalidate getOneSsoProviderById cache after SSO provider update

The edit modal loads provider details via useAuthenticationServiceGetOneSsoProviderById,
but on save only getAllSsoProviders was invalidated. Closing and reopening the
modal therefore showed stale data from the cached detail query.

Also invalidate the getOneSsoProviderById query family on update so the next
modal open re-fetches fresh configuration including updated permission mappings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(ATT-240): fix cookieSameSite i18n and API description to reflect actual behaviour

Previous descriptions were inaccurate in two ways:

1. The setting was described as controlling "session cookies" (plural), implying
   it covers all cookies. In reality it only controls the auth-session cookie.
   The oidc-state cookie is hardcoded to lax regardless of this setting.

2. The strict option claimed it "breaks SSO logins — IdP redirects will not
   carry the session cookie." This is wrong: the OIDC callback does not need
   the existing auth-session cookie (it creates a new one), and the oidc-state
   cookie that does need to survive the IdP redirect is always lax. Strict does
   not break SSO login itself. What it does break is the everyday case of users
   following links from email notifications or external sites — they will appear
   logged out even with a valid session. That makes lax the correct default.

Update EN and DE i18n strings and the Swagger DTO description accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ATT-240): remove configurable cookieSameSite, hardcode strict

Attraccess is a SPA where all API calls are same-site XHR, so
SameSite=Strict is always correct for the auth-session cookie.
The configurable setting added unnecessary complexity.

- Delete deriveCookieSecurity() and its tests
- Simplify CookieConfigService to hardcode sameSite=strict
- Remove cookieSameSite from constants, DTOs, SettingsService, and frontend
- Add migration to clean up the DB setting row
- SSO state cookies (OIDC/SAML) remain SameSite=Lax as before

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ATT-240): restore per-request OIDC strategy creation in guard

The SSOOIDCStrategy was only created once at module startup with
placeholder values. The guard fetched real provider config per request
but only passed the callback URL dynamically — the strategy still used
placeholder issuer/clientId/tokenURL causing login redirects to fail.

Restore the original pattern: create a new SSOOIDCStrategy instance
per request with the real provider config from the database. This
registers with passport under 'sso-oidc', replacing the placeholder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(ATT-240): add regression tests for per-request OIDC strategy creation

Mock SSOOIDCStrategy constructor and verify the guard creates a new
instance per request with the real provider config, callback URL, and
state store — not the placeholder values from module init. These tests
would have caught the regression where strategy creation was removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: validate CORS origin against configured app URL instead of reflecting any origin (#473)

The previous CORS configuration reflected any request origin back with
credentials enabled, allowing any website to make authenticated cross-origin
requests. Now validates against the configured app URL origin, with fallback
to permissive mode during initial setup (when no URL is configured yet).

Addresses Sourcery AI review feedback on PR #444.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Xv9LU6tqxonUKmjgwFpKhw

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Add Bismarck Brief integration to morning briefing

Adds daily Bismarck Brief article fetching and summarization to Bob (OpenClaw bot):
- New bismarck-brief skill with RSS feed integration
- Fetches latest articles from https://brief.bismarckanalysis.com/feed
- Summarizes articles using OpenRouter API (Perplexity Sonar Pro)
- Tracks seen articles to avoid duplicates across days
- Sends new articles as third Telegram message after personal & news briefs

Features:
- Configurable max articles per day (default: 2)
- State tracking to identify new articles since last run
- HTML stripping and concise 2-sentence summaries
- Substack links for easy article access

Dependencies added: feedparser for RSS parsing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add SVG export for cells and libraries (#192)

## Summary

- Add `cell_to_svg` and `library_to_svg` public functions to the `gdsr`
crate
- Render polygons, paths, boxes, text, and nodes as SVG elements with
layer-based coloring (same 16-color palette as viewer)
- Flatten cell references recursively so nested hierarchies render
correctly
- Auto-size viewport to bounding box with 5% margin
- Flip Y axis (GDS Y-up → SVG Y-down) via SVG transform group
- Escape text content for XML safety

Closes #134

## Test plan

- 11 new snapshot tests covering each element type, reference
flattening, multi-layer colors, XML escaping, empty cells, and Y-flip
transform
- Full test suite passes (691 tests)
- All pre-commit checks pass

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: gdsr
repository.description: GDSII manipulation, written in Rust.

commit.message:
Fix: Replace auto-trigger with human confirmation + add ENTRY_MISSED exit option

The previous auto-trigger wrote entry_triggered=1 to DB the moment live price
crossed entry, which falsely moved trades to Active even when the trader never
bought. This caused phantom P&L on exit.

Fix 1: Pending zone now shows "Did you place a buy order?" with Yes/No buttons.
YES confirms entry and moves to Active. NO cancels with zero P&L as ENTRY_MISSED.

Fix 2: Exit gate now has 4-way radio: WAVE_EXIT, TRAILING_STOP, ENTRY_MISSED,
MANUAL. ENTRY_MISSED closes with zero P&L and clean history record.

Fix 3: Trade History table now shows labelled reasons (Wave Exit, Stop Hit,
Entry Missed, Expired, Cancelled) instead of raw DB status codes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add agent-hardening: loud unpack failure, --dry-run, describe command

Three modifications based on Poehnelt's agent-hardening analysis:

1. unpack now errors loudly when LLM-friendly format detected instead of
   silently extracting zero files. Provides actionable remediation options.
   Added --force flag to override for intentional best-effort extraction.

2. --dry-run flag on archive shows file count, estimated tokens, and
   per-file sizes without writing any output.

3. describe subcommand dumps full CLI surface as machine-readable JSON
   for agent/tool integration (all commands or specific command).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: txtarchive
repository.description: pack and unpack an archive (directory) to a single or multiple text files that can be cut and pasted to a remote location where connections are limited.  Also provides the ability to make the archive LLM friendly so your selected code base can be provided to an LLM model

commit.message:
Switch to implicit auth flow for static site password recovery

PKCE flow (default in Supabase JS v2) requires server-side code exchange
which doesn't work on a static GitHub Pages export. Implicit flow puts
access_token and type=recovery directly in the URL hash fragment, which
the client can detect and process.

Changes:
- supabase.ts: configure flowType: 'implicit' + detectSessionInUrl
- auth-provider: detect type=recovery from hash at initialization time
  (useState initializer runs before any effect/render)
- login-form: add trailing slash to redirectTo URL to prevent 301

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate UI from Tkinter to pywebview with skeuomorphic TE design

Replace Tkinter GUI with pywebview (WKWebView on macOS) to enable
pixel-perfect CSS rendering of Teenage Engineering TP-7 inspired design.

Key changes:
- New pywebview launcher with JS<->Python bridge (api.py, bridge.js)
- Skeuomorphic UI: layered shadows, noise textures, 3-layer REC button
- Speaker diarization support in whisper_transcriber.py
- Summary markdown parser with styled sections and bold support
- Settings modal, toast notifications, progress bar
- Duration cache to avoid repeated ffprobe calls
- Bug fixes: AudioRecorder absolute paths, safe extension replacement
- Updated launchers (.command + .app bundle) for pywebview
- Old Tkinter GUI preserved as pan_sekretarz_tkinter.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Markant RALA validator errors for PDF/A-3 compliance

- Embed Bitstream Vera TTF fonts (bundled with reportlab) to fix font
  embedding requirement (ISO_19005_3#6.2.11.4.1#1)
- Add sRGB OutputIntent post-processor using pypdf to resolve DeviceRGB
  color space error (ISO_19005_3#6.2.4.3#2)
- Fix net price rounding: use gross_price - disc_amount instead of
  gross_price * (1 - pct/100) for XML/PDF/totals consistency (VD-Valitool-62)
- Add UI caption warning users to enter exact Markant-registered buyer
  legal names (BR-MARKANT-RALA-02)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
